title: Pogostemon helferi
intro: Pogostemon helferi, also known as Helfer's Patchouli, is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, commonly used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies. The health benefits of Pogostemon helferi include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as pogostone and patchouli alcohol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pogostemon helferi include essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote relaxation and well-being.
taxonomy: Pogostemon helferi is classified under the kingdom Plantae, further classified under the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, family Lamiaceae, and genus Pogostemon. The species Pogostemon helferi is the specific name given to this plant. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Pogostemon helferi is often used in aquariums as a popular aquatic plant species.

side_effects: Pogostemon helferi, also known as 'Broad Leaf Anubias', is generally considered non-toxic to aquarium inhabitants and humans, but it may cause digestive issues in some aquarium inhabitants if ingested.
